Surgical anatomy of stomach

Curvatures of stomach - lesser and greater

Orifices of stomach - cardiac and pyloric

Parts of stomach - fundus, body and pylorus

Anterior relations - liver and anterior abdominal wall

Stomach bed

Attachment of lesser omentum

Parietal cells and acid production

Attachment of greater omentum

Gastrin production and mucus

Veins of stomach drain into portal vein

Two vagus nerves - anterior and posterior are secretomotor parasympathetic to stomach

📝 Test Your Knowledge

Click on your answer to see if you're correct!

1. Which of the following forms the right border of the stomach?
  • Greater curvature
  • Cardiac notch
  • Lesser curvature
  • Fundus
2. The anterior surface of the stomach is related to:
  • Pancreas
  • Spleen
  • Left kidney
  • Liver and anterior abdominal wall
3. Which structure forms part of the stomach bed?
  • Rectus abdominis
  • Diaphragm
  • Pancreas
  • Urinary bladder
4. Parietal cells of the stomach are primarily responsible for:
  • Gastrin secretion
  • Mucus secretion
  • Pepsin production
  • Hydrochloric acid secretion
5. The venous drainage of the stomach ultimately reaches the:
  • Inferior vena cava
  • Azygos vein
  • Portal vein
  • Renal vein
6. The anterior and posterior vagus nerves supplying the stomach provide:
  • Sympathetic motor supply
  • Sensory supply only
  • Secretomotor parasympathetic supply
  • Somatic motor supply
